sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: FCV Dender come into this match with mixed results in their last 5 games, managing 1 win, 3 draws, and 1 loss, showing some resilience with late goals. KVC Westerlo, however, are struggling, winless in their last 6 matches with 2 draws and 3 losses, including failing to score in their last 3 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between FCV Dender and KVC Westerlo have been competitive, resulting in a 1-1 draw and a 4-2 win for Westerlo. Historically, games between these sides often see goals from both teams, with 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads having under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: FCV Dender, sitting 16th with 8 points, have struggled offensively with only 10 goals scored, though they excel in ground duels (54.5% won, league-best). KVC Westerlo, 12th with 16 points, have a slightly better attack (19 goals) but are vulnerable defensively, conceding 24 goals, same as Dender.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends and Insights
sections.text: Dender’s matches often see both teams scoring (7/7 recent games), while Westerlo’s games tend to have fewer goals (7/8 under 2.5). Westerlo also average over 10.5 corners in recent matches, which could play a role in set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Westerlo’s slight edge in league standings and attacking output, coupled with Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% away win chance, they are marginally favored. However, Dender’s home resilience and ability to score late could make this a tight contest, likely ending in a low-scoring affair.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, FCV Dender leads KVC Westerlo by a narrow margin of 1-0 at the Dender Football Complex. The home side managed to capitalize on a late first-half opportunity through David Toševski's well-taken goal in the 40th minute. Despite having slightly less possession, Dender made the most of their chances, while Westerlo, who controlled the ball marginally more, squandered key opportunities to level the score. The game remains finely poised with both teams showing intent in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw distinct phases of dominance in attack from both sides. Westerlo started aggressively, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes and maintaining pressure through several attacking spells early on. However, Dender gradually shifted the momentum in their favor around the midway point of the half, building sustained pressure and eventually finding the breakthrough. Towards the end of the half, Westerlo attempted to respond with renewed vigor, but Dender held firm, showing resilience while also posing threats on the counter. The ebb and flow of attacking momentum suggests both teams are capable of creating danger in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Westerlo has shown more attacking promise in terms of creating significant chances, having crafted a couple of big opportunities but failing to convert them. Their expected goals tally reflects a strong potential to score, which they will likely aim to capitalize on after the break. Dender, on the other hand, has been more clinical with fewer clear-cut chances, securing the lead through efficiency in front of goal. Both teams have been active in the final third with identical entries and penalty area penetrations, indicating an open game. Westerlo's slight edge in aerial and overall duels could play a role in their pursuit of an equalizer.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For FCV Dender, David Toševski has been the standout performer, not only for his goal but also for his involvement in the attack, earning a high rating at the break. Fabio Ferraro also contributed significantly with an assist and key defensive contributions. On the KVC Westerlo side, Isa Sakamoto had notable chances but missed critical opportunities, which could haunt his team if they fail to recover. Arthur Piedfort has been creative in midfield, looking to unlock Dender's defense with precise passing. These individuals could be pivotal in determining the outcome of the match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see KVC Westerlo come out with renewed intensity as they chase an equalizer. Given their higher expected goals and missed big chances, they have the potential to turn the game around if they can find their finishing touch. FCV Dender will aim to protect their lead, likely focusing on solid defending while looking for counter-attacking opportunities to extend their advantage. The game could open up if Westerlo pushes forward, potentially leading to more goal-scoring chances for both sides. Discipline will be key, as Dender has already picked up a yellow card, which could influence their defensive approach.
